findings.reachability: Reachable by any WordPress plugin or theme that calls BSF_Analytics_Loader::get_instance()->set_entity() with an attacker-influenced path value; BSF_Analytics_Loader is a shared singleton across all Brainstorm Force products.
findings.justification: The value stored in $this->analytics_path comes directly from $data['path'] (passed to set_entity()) with no sanitization, realpath() check, or prefix validation, and is used in require_once $this->analytics_path . '/class-bsf-analytics.php' — if any calling plugin allows an attacker-controlled value into the path field, this becomes an arbitrary file inclusion (LFI/RCE).

detection_pattern.section_name: LFI: require/include of unsanitized variable path
detection_pattern.description: Catches require/require_once/include calls using a variable path without adjacent realpath() or WordPress path-constant anchoring; such calls can become LFI or RCE if the variable is influenced by attacker-supplied data.

findings.reachability: Same trust boundary as above: any caller of set_entity() with a controllable path value; the path is concatenated directly with '/version.json' and passed to file_get_contents().
findings.justification: file_get_contents($data['path'] . '/version.json') uses an unsanitized path from set_entity() callers with no realpath() or directory-prefix check; if $data['path'] contains traversal sequences (e.g. '../../sensitive-dir'), it can read arbitrary server files, including wp-config.php.

detection_pattern.section_name: Path Traversal: file_get_contents with unsanitized variable prefix
detection_pattern.description: Catches file_get_contents calls where the path is built by concatenating a variable with a literal suffix, without any path-anchoring or traversal-prevention check; an attacker-controlled variable prefix allows reading arbitrary files.

findings.reachability: Triggered whenever the version.json file exists but does not contain the 'bsf-analytics-ver' key; reachable by any entity whose path resolves to a directory containing a version.json without that key.
findings.justification: After json_decode(), the code directly accesses $analytics_version['bsf-analytics-ver'] without an isset() guard; if the JSON is malformed or the key is absent, PHP emits a notice and $analytics_version becomes null, causing version_compare(null, ...) to behave unexpectedly and potentially allowing an attacker-placed version.json to influence which analytics path wins the version-comparison race.

detection_pattern.section_name: Missing array key guard after json_decode
detection_pattern.description: Catches patterns where json_decode output is immediately indexed by a literal key without an isset() or array_key_exists() guard; missing keys produce PHP notices and null values that can corrupt downstream logic such as version comparisons.
